com: Formal offer by means of scheme of arrangement announced at 590p per share plus 22 pence dividend. “Hard” irrevocable undertakings of 42% place Nuveen well to reach 75% of votes needed.
Scheme of arrangement made at same terms as indicative proposal. Expected to become effective during H2 2026.
Approved by target shareholders. Completion still subject to regulatory approvals, with a long stop date of 31 July 2026.
Shareholders have overwhelmingly approved the scheme, but still subject to regulatory approval. Completion expected some time in Q3.

Talks ongoing with three parties: Advent International, Tinicum/Blackstone Consortium and Arcline Investment Management. On 5 March 2026, Advent announced a 270p proposal, plus 2p final dividend. As it's at a sizeable discount to current share price, will likely need to go higher. Hedge fund % change adjusted for opening position declared today.
Competing approaches from Bridgepoint and Triton, following a strategic review. PUSU Deadline extended on 18 Feb 2026 as discussions ongoing.
Strategic review after meetings with financial investors who wanted to maximise value. No approaches mentioned.
Shareholders approved scheme on 11 March. Still subject to regulatory approval, with parties aiming to complete by end of Q2 2026.
Strategic review ongoing - NCC has not stated it has received an approach.
Strategic review and formal sales process. No approaches mentioned.
Strategic review and formal sales process. On 11 Feb 2026, in response to media speculation interest from LondonMetric Property PLC confirmed. No PUSU deadline.

Bidder has switched from scheme to takeover offer to "increase certainty of execution". Bidder's support for offer in respect of 50.14% is above the minimum acceptance condition of 50%. On 20 Feb 2026, timetable suspended due to outstanding regulatory conditions. Completion expected in Q2.
Consortium has gone hostile. Board had rejected indicative offer, calling it highly opportunistic and fundamentally undervaluing company.
Offer, by scheme of arrangement, from Qatar-based bank, expected to complete Q3. Undertakings of 19.5%. Offer follows strategic review addressing "persistent discount to its realisable asset value".

Multiple unsolicited proposals from Saudi Arabia's Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah, which has due diligence access. No price given. Discussions were ongoing and the board was seeking clarity around Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah's funding arrangements.
Scheme of arrangement requiring 75% of votes. Undertakings & letters of intent for just 9.22%. Completion expected Q2.
Strategic review, has received approaches from two parties.
Offer seems to be proceeding as planned. Scheme meetings scheduled for 19 March 2026.
Strategic review, with a variety of strategic options being considered. No approaches mentioned.
Expected to complete at some point in H1 2026. Shareholders voted in favour on 12 March 2026.
Switched from scheme to takeover offer on 20 Feb 2026, “in order to increase certainty”. Offer unconditional on 13 March 2026, with 50.1% acceptance.

Discussions part of a strategic review. IQE was in receipt of an approach from a potential offeror, although there could be no certainty either that an offer would be made nor as to the terms of any offer.
Approved by target shareholders. Completion still subject to FCA approvals, with a long stop date of 29 March 2026.
At “minded to recommend” stage. We expect a formal offer.
Shareholders have overwhelmingly approved the scheme. FCA approval received, with completion expected on 24 March 2026.
Discussions are with selected third parties as part of a private strategic review process.
17p offer is less than 20p proposal, but strong undertakings in respect of 57%. Unconditional date 28 May 2026.

Approved by target shareholders, completion has been delayed by regulatory approvals, namely from North Sea Transition Authority. On 27 Feb, Deltic remarked on the "ongoing uncertainty and significant length of time that this regulatory process is taking". NSTA unlikely to be able to conclude its deliberations until late March 2026, and "could not commit to any firm timeline".
